## §1 RESEARCH CONCLUSION

THESIS-TRACKING. The company is expected to complete its deleveraging programme by end of year,
returning to a 1.0x net debt/EBITDA target. Management has guided to a resumption of share buybacks
following the Q3 results. The dividend growth trajectory of 8–10% per annum is consistent with
M&A integration synergies and capex discipline in the base case.

## §2 EVIDENCE THAT WOULD CHANGE THE CONCLUSION

1. Net debt/EBITDA remains above 2.5x after the planned divestment programme.
2. Buyback programme is cancelled or deferred due to covenant restrictions.
3. Dividend cover falls below 1.5x on a cash basis.

## §3 MONITORING AND RE-UNDERWRITING PLAN

| Variable | Source | Frequency | Trigger | Stage to re-run | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Net debt/EBITDA | quarterly results | quarterly | above 2.5x | C3, C7 | review leverage thesis |
| Buyback announcements | RNS / investor relations | event-driven | cancellation or deferral | B1, C2 | revise FCF and returns |
